results.title: Toyota FY2025 Results: Net Revenue ¥48.0 Trillion, Operating Income ¥4.8 Trillion
results.snippet: Toyota Motor Corporation reported FY2025 (year ended March 2025) net revenues of ¥48.0 trillion, up 9% year-over-year. Operating income reached ¥4.8 trillion (+3%), a record for the third consecutive year. Consolidated vehicle sales were 10.87 million units globally. Net income attributable to Toyota shareholders was ¥3.6 trillion. The company declared an annual dividend of ¥125 per share.

results.title: Toyota Hybrid Sales Hit 40% of Global Volume; bZ4X and Solid-State Battery Timeline
results.snippet: Hybrid vehicles, including HEV and PHEV, represented 40% of Toyota's global unit sales in FY2025, up from 28% three years ago. Toyota and Lexus sold 3.8 million hybrid units in FY2025, more than all other automakers combined. CEO Koji Sato confirmed Toyota's solid-state battery commercialisation target of 2027-2028, enabling EVs with 1,200km range and 10-minute charging.

results.title: Toyota Woven City Smart City Opens Phase 1 in Susono, Japan
results.snippet: Toyota's Woven City, a fully connected living laboratory at the base of Mount Fuji, opened its Phase 1 to the first 360 residents in early 2025. The facility tests autonomous vehicles, robotics, smart grid energy, and AI-assisted healthcare in a real-world environment. Woven City is built on the site of a former Toyota manufacturing plant in Susono, Shizuoka Prefecture, and represents a ¥100 billion investment.
